title: EEG Study of Interoceptive Precision Gating
description: This protocol examines whether interoceptive precision (Πⁱ), indexed by the heartbeat-evoked potential (HEP, 250–400 ms window), modulates conscious detection thresholds (θₜ). Stimuli are time-locked to cardiac phase to test four sub-predictions: (Pred 1.A) that state-level Πⁱ_eff produces greater P3b amplitude for near-threshold stimuli under interoceptive focus than exteroceptive focus or dual-task control — with the effect absent or reversed for suprathreshold stimuli; (Pred 1.A-trait) that trait-level Πⁱ_baseline, indexed by interoceptive accuracy (IA), predicts greater perceptual sensitivity (d′) in the interoceptive condition but not the exteroceptive condition; (Pred 1.B) that cardiac-phase-locked detection advantage exists at diastole versus systole; and (Pred 1.C) that top-tertile IA participants show the strongest state-level P3b condition effects and cardiac-phase detection advantages. Near-threshold contrast titration is mandatory; suprathreshold control blocks are required to confirm specificity.
prediction_cluster_label: Interoceptive Precision Modulates Detection Threshold (State and Trait Levels)

sub_predictions.claim: State-level Πⁱ_eff → P3b: interoceptive focus produces greater P3b for near-threshold stimuli than exteroceptive focus or dual-task control. Near-threshold titration mandatory; effect must be absent or reversed for suprathreshold stimuli.
sub_predictions.confirming_evidence: P3b amplitude: interoceptive > exteroceptive > dual-task (p < 0.05 FWE; ηₚ² ≥ 0.06). Effect absent or reversed in suprathreshold matched control block. HEP predicts P3b (partial r > 0.4 surviving RMSSD and pupil diameter covariate control).
sub_predictions.falsifying_evidence: No significant P3b condition main effect (all p > 0.10) across N ≥ 30 in preregistered contrast; or HEP–P3b partial correlation < r = 0.20 after arousal covariate control.

confirming_thresholds.suprathreshold_control: required — effect must be absent or reversed

sub_predictions.claim: Trait-level Πⁱ_baseline → d′: high-IA (top tertile) shows greater perceptual sensitivity (d′) than low-IA (bottom tertile), specific to interoceptive detection condition. Group × modality dissociation control required.
sub_predictions.confirming_evidence: High-IA d′ > Low-IA d′ (independent-samples t or Mann–Whitney, p < 0.05; Cohen's d ≥ 0.5); group difference specific to interoceptive condition, absent in matched exteroceptive condition (group × modality interaction p < 0.05).
sub_predictions.falsifying_evidence: High-IA and Low-IA do not differ on interoceptive d′ (d < 0.3 across N ≥ 30 per group); or d′ group difference replicates equally in the exteroceptive condition, eliminating interoceptive specificity.

confirming_thresholds.tertile_grouping: top vs. bottom tertile on IA (heartbeat counting accuracy)

falsifying_thresholds.condition: Group difference replicates equally in exteroceptive condition (no interaction)

sub_predictions.claim: Cardiac-phase-locked detection advantage at diastole (300–500 ms post-R-peak) vs. systole (0–150 ms post-R-peak).
sub_predictions.confirming_evidence: Logistic mixed-effects model: detection ~ cardiac_phase × contrast + cardiac_phase | participant; significant cardiac_phase × contrast interaction (p < 0.05). d′ higher at diastole than systole.
sub_predictions.falsifying_evidence: Diastole vs. systole hit rate advantage < 5% across two independent samples (interaction p > 0.15); no d′ difference between cardiac phases.

sub_predictions.claim: Top-tertile IA participants show strongest Pred 1.A (state P3b condition effect) and Pred 1.B (cardiac-phase detection advantage) effects.
sub_predictions.confirming_evidence: Accuracy × condition interaction on P3b amplitude significant (p < 0.05, ηₚ² ≥ 0.08); high-accuracy group shows larger P3b condition difference than low-accuracy group. Accuracy × cardiac-phase interaction on detection rate also significant (p < 0.05).
sub_predictions.falsifying_evidence: Accuracy × condition interaction p > 0.10; tertile groups do not differ in cardiac-phase detection advantage.

confirming_thresholds.tertile_grouping: top vs. bottom tertile on IA

validation_notes: Formally specified. Parameter identifiability confirmed via simulation (Appendix A.4). Identifiability of Πⁱ from HEP confirmed via block-diagonal Fisher Information Matrix. Pred 1.A-trait and Pred 1.C added to match registry EP-1 requirements; suprathreshold control blocks added as mandatory.

inclusion_criteria: Resting heart rate 50–100 BPM with regular sinus rhythm
No history of cardiac arrhythmia or autonomic neuropathy
exclusion_criteria: Irregular ECG rhythm (assessed by study nurse at screening)
History of neurological or psychiatric disorder (DSM-5 criteria)
Use of beta-blockers or other HR-modifying medication
Inability to maintain fixation for 30-minute EEG blocks

apparatus.EEG: 64-channel BrainAmp DC (Brain Products GmbH) at 1000 Hz, referenced to FCz, re-referenced offline to average mastoids
apparatus.ECG: BrainAmp ExG bipolar lead-II at 1000 Hz, synchronised to EEG clock
apparatus.display: VPixx ProPixx at 120 Hz, viewing distance 57 cm, gamma-corrected
apparatus.stimulus_delivery: Psychtoolbox-3 (MATLAB R2024b) with photodiode verification of onset timing
apparatus.pupillometry: Eyelink 1000 Plus (monocular, 500 Hz) for arousal covariate control (Pred 1.A)
apparatus.cardiac_phase_algorithm: R-peak detection via Pan-Tompkins (real-time), diastole window 300–500 ms post R-peak, systole window 0–150 ms post R-peak
apparatus.ia_measure: Heartbeat counting task (Schandry 1981) administered pre-scan: 6 intervals × 2 conditions; d′ computed as IA index for tertile grouping (Pred 1.A-trait, 1.C)

conditions: interoceptive_focus (attend to heartbeat while detecting stimulus)
exteroceptive_focus (attend to external tone while detecting stimulus)
dual_task_control (attend to both heartbeat and tone simultaneously)
trial_structure.response: 2AFC (seen / not seen), response window 1500 ms, response-locked to keypress

blocks.suprathreshold_control_block: 2 additional blocks (60 trials each) at suprathreshold contrast for Pred 1.A specificity test
blocks.condition_counterbalancing: Cardiac phase pseudo-randomised across trials within block; attention condition (interoceptive/exteroceptive/dual-task) counterbalanced across blocks

eeg_analysis_pipeline.ica_components: Automated rejection of cardiac and ocular ICA components (EEGLAB ADJUST)

dependent_variables: P3b amplitude (300–600 ms, Pz) per attention condition (interoceptive / exteroceptive / dual-task) — primary (Pred 1.A)
P3b amplitude × condition ANOVA: ηₚ² for condition main effect — primary (Pred 1.A)
HEP amplitude (250–400 ms, Cz) per attention condition
HEP–P3b partial correlation (r), controlling for RMSSD and pupil diameter — Pred 1.A specificity
P3b condition effect at suprathreshold contrast — Pred 1.A control block
d-prime (signal detection theory) per attention condition and IA tertile group — Pred 1.A-trait
Group × modality interaction on d′ (high-IA vs. low-IA × interoceptive vs. exteroceptive) — Pred 1.A-trait
Detection rate per cardiac phase (systole vs. diastole) — Pred 1.B
IA (interoceptive accuracy) score per participant — tertile assignment (Pred 1.C)
Accuracy × attention-condition interaction on P3b amplitude — Pred 1.C
Accuracy × cardiac-phase interaction on detection rate — Pred 1.C
primary_hypothesis: Interoceptive focus produces greater P3b amplitude for near-threshold stimuli than exteroceptive focus or dual-task control (three-way condition difference, ηₚ² ≥ 0.06), this effect is absent at suprathreshold contrast, and HEP amplitude predicts P3b with partial r > 0.4 surviving arousal covariate control. High-IA participants show larger P3b condition effects and diastolic detection advantages than low-IA participants.
primary_analysis: Repeated-measures ANOVA: P3b_amplitude ~ condition (interoceptive/exteroceptive/dual-task) × contrast (near/supra-threshold) × cardiac_phase, with HRV and pupil diameter as covariates. FWE-correction across electrode clusters. HEP–P3b partial correlation via mixed-effects with RMSSD and pupil as fixed covariates. IA tertile × condition interaction for Pred 1.C.

power_analysis.based_on: Garfinkel et al. (2014) cardiac-phase detection effect; Canales-Johnson et al. (2020) HEP–P3b coupling

caveats: Pred 1.A requires three attention conditions; dual-task control condition substantially increases session length — practice blocks must ensure participants understand the dual-task instruction.
Suprathreshold control blocks are mandatory for Pred 1.A specificity; failure to include them means Pred 1.A cannot be confirmed per registry criteria.
IA tertile grouping (Pred 1.A-trait, 1.C) requires pre-scan heartbeat counting; this must be administered identically across sites if multi-site.
